title: Multi-Domain Network Health Executive Dashboard
dataSources: Multi-domain composite dashboard. Primary: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:networkhealth`. from the `networkhealth` modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538) polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/network-health`. Default poll interval: 900s (15m). Volume: ~1 event per poll (aggregate network score). RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `healthScore` (integer 0-100), `goodCount` (integer), `badCount` (integer), `totalCount` (integer). Additional sources: `index=sdwan sourcetype=cisco:sdwan:*` (SD-WAN), `index=meraki sourcetype=meraki:api` (Meraki), `index=thousandeyes sourcetype=thousandeyes:*` (ThousandEyes). Each source requires its respective TA and data collection configuration.
app: `Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk` (Splunkbase 7538) + `Cisco ThousandEyes App for Splunk` (Splunkbase 7719) + `Cisco Meraki Add-on for Splunk` (Splunkbase 5580)
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:networkhealth" | stats latest(healthScore) as campus_health | appendcols [search index=sdwan sourcetype="cisco:sdwan:*" | stats latest(health_score) as wan_health] | appendcols [search index=cisco_network sourcetype="meraki:api" | stats avg(health_score) as branch_health] | appendcols [search `stream_index` thousandeyes.test.type="agent-to-server" | stats avg(network.latency) as te_latency_s | eval te_latency_ms=round(te_latency_s*1000,1)] | eval overall_health=round((campus_health+coalesce(wan_health,campus_health)+coalesce(branch_health,campus_health))/3,1) | table campus_health wan_health branch_health te_latency_ms overall_health
description: Provides a unified executive dashboard combining health scores from Catalyst Center (campus), SD-WAN (WAN), Meraki (branch), and ThousandEyes (external paths) into a single multi-domain view.
value: Executives need one view of network health, not four consoles. This dashboard combines all Cisco network domains into a single composite health score.
implementation:
1. **7538 (Catalyst, SD-WAN, optional Cyber Vision):** Configure Catalyst Center + vManage per UC-5.13.1 / UC-5.13.16 / UC-5.13.69.
2. **5580 (Meraki):** Org API to `meraki:api` in `index=cisco_network` (UC-5.13.70).
3. **7719 (ThousandEyes):** OTel stream + **`stream_index`** macro for agent-to-server tests (UC-5.13.71).
4. **Composite:** The `coalesce` terms avoid null WAN/branch in partial deployments; replace with 0 or separate panels if you need strict math.
5. **Dashboard:** Dashboard Studio (or Simple XML) with one row per domain + headline single values for `overall_health` and `te_latency_ms`.
visualization: Executive row: large single values for campus_health, wan_health, branch_health, te_latency_ms, overall_health; treemap of domain status; link-out drilldowns to UC-5.13.68–5.13.72 panel searches.
knownFalsePositives:
**One domain's data source offline making the multi-domain dashboard incomplete.** If one of the data sources (SD-WAN, Meraki, ThousandEyes) is not configured or temporarily unavailable, its health score will be null or missing. Distinguish by checking whether the missing score corresponds to a data source that is not deployed in this environment. Suppress by using `| fillnull value="N/A"` for undeployed data sources and noting which domains are active in the dashboard header.

**Different health score scales across domains making side-by-side comparison misleading.** Campus (0-100), WAN (may use different scale), and Meraki (different methodology) health scores may not be directly comparable. Distinguish by checking the typical range for each domain — if they cluster at different ranges, direct comparison is inappropriate. Suppress by normalizing all scores to a common scale (e.g., percentile rank within each domain) before presenting in the executive dashboard.

**Maintenance window in one domain creating a dip that the executive dashboard shows as a cross-domain problem.** A WAN circuit maintenance may cause the WAN health column to drop while all other domains are healthy. Distinguish by checking whether only one domain is affected. Suppress by adding per-domain maintenance window annotations to the dashboard.

**Executive audience misinterpreting a single-domain dip as a systemic issue.** The multi-domain dashboard is designed for executive consumption. A localized issue in one domain may be interpreted as a broader problem. No SPL suppression — mitigate by adding contextual annotations (trend sparklines, domain-specific drill-downs) so the executive can see that the issue is contained.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) with devicehealth, clienthealth, and networkhealth inputs enabled to `index=catalyst`.
• Additional cross-product data sources optional: SD-WAN (`index=sdwan`), Meraki (`index=meraki`), ThousandEyes (`index=thousandeyes`), ISE (`index=ise`).
• RBAC: all referenced TA service accounts must have appropriate read access.
• Executive audience: this dashboard is designed for CIO/CTO-level consumers — optimize for clarity over detail.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
This UC does not require additional data collection. It aggregates and summarizes data from multiple existing Catalyst Center sourcetypes and optionally from cross-product data sources.

Sourcetypes consumed:
• `cisco:dnac:devicehealth` — infrastructure health scores.
• `cisco:dnac:clienthealth` — client experience scores.
• `cisco:dnac:networkhealth` — network health scores.
• `cisco:dnac:issue` — active issues and priorities.
• `cisco:dnac:securityadvisory` — active advisory counts.
• Optionally: SD-WAN, Meraki, ThousandEyes, ISE data for multi-domain view.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

```spl
index=catalyst (sourcetype="cisco:dnac:devicehealth" OR sourcetype="cisco:dnac:clienthealth" OR sourcetype="cisco:dnac:networkhealth")
| eval domain=case(sourcetype="cisco:dnac:devicehealth","Infrastructure", sourcetype="cisco:dnac:clienthealth","Client Experience", sourcetype="cisco:dnac:networkhealth","Network")
| stats avg(overallHealth) as avg_health min(overallHealth) as min_health dc(deviceName) as entity_count by domain
| eval status=case(avg_health>=75,"Healthy", avg_health>=50,"Fair", avg_health>=25,"Degraded", 1==1,"Critical")
| append [search index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:issue" status="ACTIVE" priority IN ("P1","P2") | stats count as active_p1p2 | eval domain="Active P1/P2 Issues"]
| append [search index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:securityadvisory" severity IN ("CRITICAL","HIGH") deviceCount>0 | stats dc(advisoryId) as active_critical_advisories | eval domain="Security Advisories"]
| table domain, avg_health, status, entity_count, active_p1p2, active_critical_advisories
```

Understanding this SPL:
• **Three-domain health aggregation:** Infrastructure (devices), Client Experience (users), and Network (overall network health) — the three pillars of Catalyst Center Assurance.
• **`avg(overallHealth)` and `min(overallHealth)`**: Shows both average and worst-case health per domain. The average provides the executive summary; the minimum identifies the worst-performing entity.
• **`append` commands**: Add context from issue and advisory data — active P1/P2 issues and critical advisories provide the "attention needed" signal.
• **`status=case(...)`**: Uses the same health banding as Catalyst Center Assurance (75/50/25) for consistent interpretation.

Step 3 — Validate
• **UI comparison:** Open Catalyst Center > Assurance > Health Dashboard. The three health summary tiles (Device, Client, Network) should match the Splunk domain averages within ±2%.
• **Issue count check:** Compare the active P1/P2 issue count with Catalyst Center > Assurance > Issues filtered to P1+P2.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• **Dashboard layout:** Three large single-value tiles (Infrastructure, Client, Network health) at the top with color-coding (green/yellow/orange/red). Below: active P1/P2 issues count and critical advisories count. Bottom: 7-day health trend timechart per domain.
• **Refresh:** Schedule auto-refresh every 15 minutes (matching the TA poll interval).
• **Drill-down:** Each domain tile should link to the detailed dashboard (UC-5.13.1 for infrastructure, UC-5.13.12 for clients, UC-5.13.20 for network).

Step 5 — Troubleshoot
• **Health scores showing 0 across all domains:** The TA is not collecting data. Check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd "TA_cisco_catalyst"` for errors.
• **Executive dashboard showing stale data:** Check the data freshness using UC-5.13.74 (Data Collection Health).
• **Cross-product data not appearing:** Verify the additional TAs (SD-WAN, Meraki, etc.) are installed and collecting data.
grandmaExplanation: We combine the health of your entire network — campus, branches, wide-area connections, and internet paths — into one executive view so leadership can see the big picture.
